affectedDomains: Privacy / Consent / Retention / Data Subject Rights
Audit and Activity (adjacent, not directly touched)

conflictRisks.hazard: new purpose-mapping logic added to the domain with the highest fan-out (106 edges) and 9 X-severity boundary violations per F0-004
conflictRisks.mitigation: treat new purpose map as unaccepted contract pending ADR-015 review
conflictRisks.requiredSequencing: continuation merged before any WhatsApp boundary-violation remediation begins

conflictRisks.hazard: consent-check logic changing concurrently with any SMS architecture review
conflictRisks.mitigation: treat current SMS consent behavior as unverified until continuation merges

conflictRisks.hazard: new consent-gate test implies recall candidate generation becoming consent-aware mid-change
conflictRisks.mitigation: do not assume recall respects consent in either direction until continuation merges

conflictRisks.hazard: retention cleanup acting on consent/audit rows during schema transition risks deleting or corrupting data the reconciliation logic still needs
conflictRisks.mitigation: no independent deployment of retention schedule/scope changes outside the continuation unit
conflictRisks.requiredSequencing: continuation merged, retention job behavior independently re-verified post-merge

conflictRisks.hazard: cross-domain messaging bypassing consent public contract mid-implementation
conflictRisks.mitigation: no assumption that messages.ts currently consent-gates all sends
conflictRisks.requiredSequencing: continuation merged, gate behavior verified across all channels

conflictRisks.hazard: broad blast radius; any concurrent schema-level architecture work touching the same tables would conflict
conflictRisks.mitigation: already covered by program-wide freeze; F0-007 adds no new schema-touching authorization
conflictRisks.requiredSequencing: continuation merged; F0-009 remains design-only regardless

correctionPass.reason: External review of PR #174 found the document set stated the primary tree's branch/HEAD were unchanged throughout the task, and called the continuation flatly 'uncommitted, no PR' without time-slicing. Both corrected here and across KVKK_ACTIVE_WORK_BASELINE.md, KVKK_ARCHITECTURE_FREEZE_BOUNDARY.md, and F0-007_KVKK_BASELINE_EVIDENCE.md.
